verses.text: The LORD spake unto Joshua, saying: Speak to the children of Israel, saying, Appoint out for you cities of refuge, whereof I spake unto you by the hand of Moses: that the slayer that killeth any person unawares and unwittingly may flee thither: they shall be your refuge from the avenger of blood. Guidance: After the long lists of inheritance comes a list of refuges. Land for the strong is paired with sanctuary for the broken. The God who allots fields also allots safe-houses for those whose lives have gone wrong. Note 'whereof I spake by the hand of Moses' (Num 35, Deut 19) — the law of refuge had been recorded for decades; Joshua finally executes it. Mercy in scripture is not improvised; it is pre-positioned.

The God who allots fields also allots safe-houses for those whose lives have gone wrong. The LORD spake unto Joshua, saying: Speak to the children of Israel, saying, Appoint out for you cities of refuge, whereof I spake unto you by the hand of Moses: that the slayer that killeth any person unawares and unwittingly may flee thither: they shall be your refuge from the avenger of blood. Guidance: After the long lists of inheritance comes a list of refuges. Land for the strong is paired with sanctuary for the broken. The God who allots fields also allots safe-houses for those whose lives have gone wrong. Note 'whereof I spake by the hand of Moses' (Num 35, Deut 19) — the law of refuge had been recorded for decades; Joshua finally executes it. Mercy in scripture is not improvised; it is pre-positioned.

When he that doth flee unto one of those cities shall stand at the entering of the gate of the city, and shall declare his cause in the ears of the elders of that city, they shall take him into the city unto them, and give him a place, that he may dwell among them. If the avenger of blood pursue after him, then they shall not deliver the slayer up into his hand; because he smote his neighbour unwittingly, and hated him not beforetime. He shall dwell in that city, until he stand before the congregation for judgment, and until the death of the high priest that shall be in those days: then shall the slayer return, and come unto his own city, and unto his own house, unto the city from whence he fled. Guidance: The procedure is detailed: the runner declares his case at the gate; elders give him a place; he stays until trial; if accidental, he remains until the death of the high priest. A high priest's death frees the manslayer to go home — the only death in the old covenant that releases another's confinement. Hebrews 6:18 echoes the image: 'we have fled for refuge.' One greater High Priest will die, and prisoners will go free in His passing.

They appointed Kedesh in Galilee in mount Naphtali, Shechem in mount Ephraim, Kirjath-arba (which is Hebron) in the mountain of Judah. On the other side Jordan by Jericho eastward, they assigned Bezer in the wilderness upon the plain out of the tribe of Reuben, Ramoth in Gilead out of the tribe of Gad, Golan in Bashan out of the tribe of Manasseh. Guidance: Six cities, three on each side of the Jordan, geographically distributed so no fugitive is more than a day's run from one. The placement is mercy by infrastructure. The names ring with meaning — Kedesh ('holy'), Shechem ('shoulder'), Hebron ('fellowship'), Bezer ('fortress'), Ramoth ('heights'), Golan ('joy'). The accidental killer flees, and the very names whisper what awaits: holiness, fellowship, fortress, joy. God paves the road to grace with destination signs.

They appointed Kedesh in Galilee in mount Naphtali, Shechem in mount Ephraim, Kirjath-arba (which is Hebron) in the mountain of Judah. On the other side Jordan by Jericho eastward, they assigned Bezer in the wilderness upon the plain out of the tribe of Reuben, Ramoth in Gilead out of the tribe of Gad, Golan in Bashan out of the tribe of Manasseh. Guidance: Six cities, three on each side of the Jordan, geographically distributed so no fugitive is more than a day's run from one. The placement is mercy by infrastructure. The names ring with meaning — Kedesh ('holy'), Shechem ('shoulder'), Hebron ('fellowship'), Bezer ('fortress'), Ramoth ('heights'), Golan ('joy'). The accidental killer flees, and the very names whisper what awaits: holiness, fellowship, fortress, joy. God paves the road to grace with destination signs.

These were the cities appointed for all the children of Israel, and for the stranger that sojourneth among them, that whosoever killeth any person at unawares might flee thither, and not die by the hand of the avenger of blood, until he stood before the congregation. Guidance: 'And for the stranger that sojourneth among them' — the chapter's most striking word. The cities of refuge are not just for Israelites; they are for the foreigner walking through. God's mercy is the same shape regardless of passport. From Rahab to Gibeon, from Joshua 8's reading of the law to 'the women and the strangers,' the book has consistently kept the door open. Inheritance was tribal; refuge is everyone's.
